medications.indication_rationale: Regular analgesia; source advised 1000mg up to four times daily.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 1000mg orally up to four times daily (max 4g/day) (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Short course for pain/inflammation; source advised 400mg with food for 3–5 days.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 400mg orally with food (e.g. TID) for 3–5 days (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Heat/massage in the first 72h reopens bleeding vessels and worsens the haematoma.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: RICE for 48–72h (relative rest, ice with a cloth barrier, compression bandage, elevation); avoid deep heat/massage early; then gentle stretching and light walking as pain allows.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Limits bleeding/swelling then restores movement; avoids re-bleeding and stiffness.

patient_education_points.point: This is a deep bruise (contusion) of the thigh muscle — blunt force broke small vessels inside the muscle. It's benign and heals over 2–4 weeks; the bruise will change colour as it clears.
Use rest, ice (with a cloth), compression and elevation for the first couple of days, and avoid heat or hard massage early. Then start gentle movement and light walking as pain allows.

Go to ED / call 000 if the whole thigh becomes tensely, rock-hard swollen with pain far worse than expected or numbness/tingling, if the foot turns cold or pale, if you become unable to bear weight, or (over the following days) if the calf becomes swollen, warm and tender (possible clot).

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Resolution of swelling; a persistent firm lump (organising haematoma/myositis ossificans); rehab.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Compartment/fracture features — ED/000; calf swelling/warmth (DVT) over days — GP/ED.

must_include_items: Exclude femoral fracture and acute compartment syndrome before conservative management
RICE + analgesia + graded mobilisation; avoid early heat/massage
Compartment-syndrome / fracture safety-net (pain out of proportion, tense limb, numbness, cold foot, can't weight-bear)
DVT safety-net over the following days
acceptable_alternatives: Paracetamol and/or NSAID; compression bandage; physiotherapy if slow
errors_of_commission: Missing compartment syndrome / a femoral fracture
Sending an uncomplicated contusion with a negative screen to ED by ambulance
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires a fracture/compartment exclusion, RICE + graded mobilisation (no early heat/massage), and a compartment-syndrome safety-net.
